title: ophrys fusca var. atlantica
intro: Ophrys fusca var. atlantica, also known as the Atlantic Bee Orchid, is a subspecies of the Ophrys fusca, a type of orchid native to the Iberian Peninsula and the Atlantic coast of Europe. This plant is used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its chemical compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Ophrys fusca var. atlantica include its ability to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, as well as its antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections. Herbal preparations of Ophrys fusca var. atlantica are typically made by infusing the plant's flowers or roots in water or ethanol, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, or topical creams for medicinal purposes.
